since_last: While you were between cycles I kept the idle Spark productive and built the end-to-end serving hedge. Full picture below; your two balls (v6 deploy+launch, real-model ablation A/B/C) are unchanged.

downproj_lever_result.what: Cert-only (no train) mixed-precision lever: hold down_proj in bf16, re-measure v5 @ n=1024 (cert-v5-downproj-frontier).
downproj_lever_result.table: full-NVFP4: raw 0.8975, kl 0.0551 FAIL, mem 3.30x, 95%-robust cov 0.6426 @ ans 0.9970 (floor 0.9831). down_proj-bf16: raw 0.9111, kl 0.0429 PASS, mem 1.90x, 95%-robust cov 0.7012 @ ans 0.9889 (floor 0.9718).
downproj_lever_result.verdict: Valid but memory-expensive lever: +6pts robust coverage and fixes mean_kl, but gives back ~42% of compression (3.30x->1.90x). NOT a v6 substitute. For low-RAM the full-NVFP4 @ 3.30x/64% is the better byte-efficient point. v6 (or a smarter mixed scheme) is still the path to real coverage gains without the memory cost.

serving_decoder_built.what: serving/abstaining_decoder.py: AbstainingDecoder(policy) enforces the abstention policy in the token loop over a caller-supplied quant logits_provider (only model touch-point, no FP model). Confident -> emit argmax; low-margin -> emit ABSTAIN_TOKEN (defer/flag/route). Live coverage + stop_on_coverage_below circuit-breaker so a mostly-abstained answer fails loud.
serving_decoder_built.test: tests/test_abstention_serve.py now 8/8. This is the deployable shape of v5+abstention end-to-end; drop it into your serving stack with policy_from_cert(cert, confidence=0.95).

recipe_update: conformal-abstention-serve promoted candidate->VALIDATED on the real v5 n=1024 evidence (ablationDelta +0.0995 answered-over-raw at the robust point). adopted stays FALSE — lint flags it 'eligible to adopt'; the OWNER flips adopted. Do not flip it yourself.
YOUR_BALL_v6: Unchanged and still the big one: git-MERGE the v6 code path into spark-bridge PRESERVING the Spark device_map={'':0} hardening (see prior handoff for the 4 steps + the exact v6 run command with owner approval). CERT_NEVAL=1024 so v6's cert is apples-to-apples with these v5 numbers. Dry-run to confirm the v6 flags appear in B1 before --execute.
YOUR_BALL_ablation: Also unchanged: A) replication_pack on your 70B + verifiability_model.decide; B) counterfactual_traps intervention-consistency; C) novel-knowability generalization. The only path to promote any world-model prototype candidate->adopted.
guardrails: canClaimAGI=false; abstention is a MEASURED hedge (v5 ships at ~64% coverage), not a capability claim; v6 --run-train authorized ONCE by owner; merge don't clobber; owner flips adopted; lint_claims + lint_recipe before receipt commits; code only to work branch + spark-bridge deploys; no plaintext secrets.
